front.title: Payment Extension for Model Context Protocol

abstract.t: This document defines a payment extension for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling MCP tools to require payment before execution. The extension mirrors the semantics of the "Payment" HTTP authentication scheme [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment], adapting its challenge-response flow to MCP's JSON-RPC-based request-response model.
The extension introduces error code -32402 for payment challenges, and defines root-level authorization, receipt, and paymentAuthorization fields for requests and responses.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) [MCP] enables AI applications to interact with external tools and resources through a standardized JSON-RPC-based interface. This specification extends MCP to support payment-gated tool invocations, enabling MCP servers to require payment before executing tools.

This specification is designed as a companion to the "Payment" HTTP authentication scheme [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment]. Both specifications share the same payment method ecosystem and credential formats, allowing implementations to support both HTTP and MCP transports with minimal additional complexity.

section.name: Relationship to HTTP Payment Scheme
section.t: This specification intentionally mirrors the HTTP Payment scheme:
Payment method specifications (e.g., Tempo, Lightning, Solana) define credential formats that work identically across both transports.

ul.li: Paid MCP Tools: Tool providers can monetize individual tool calls
AI Agent Spending: Agents can autonomously pay for tool access
Micropayments: Per-call pricing for compute, data, or API access
Multi-Provider: Agents interact with multiple paid tool providers

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

section.name: Terminology
section.t: This specification uses terminology from [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment]:

dd.t: An error response with code -32402 containing payment requirements.
The authorization field containing payment proof.

section.t: Additional terms:

dd.t: An MCP tool that requires payment for execution.
An AI application or agent that invokes MCP tools.
A service that provides MCP tools, potentially requiring payment.

section.name: Request Flow
section.artwork: MCP Client MCP Server │ │ │ (1) {"method": "tools/call", │ │ "params": {"name": "image_generate"}} │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────────>│ │ │ │ (2) {"error": {"code": -32402, │ │ "data": "<base64url-challenge>"}} │ │<────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ (3) Client decodes challenge, fulfills payment │ │ (signs transaction, pays invoice, etc.) │ │ │ │ (4) {"method": "tools/call", │ │ "params": {"name": "image_generate"}, │ │ "authorization": "Payment <credential>"} │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────────>│ │ │ │ (5) Server verifies and settles │ │ │ │ (6) {"result": {"content": [...]}, │ │ "receipt": "<base64url>"} │ │<────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │

section.name: Error Codes
section.t: This specification reserves the following error codes:

section.name: -32402 Payment Required
section.t: Indicates the method requires payment. The error.data field MUST contain a base64url-encoded payment challenge (Section 6).
Servers MUST return -32402 when:

ul.li: A payment-gated tool is called without an authorization field
A payment-gated tool is called with an expired authorization

section.name: -32401 Payment Failed
section.t: Indicates the payment credential was well-formed but verification failed. The error.data field MUST contain a fresh base64url-encoded payment challenge.
Servers MUST return -32401 when:

ul.li: The credential signature is invalid
The payment amount is insufficient
The payment was already used (replay)
The challenge id is unknown or expired

section.t: This distinction allows clients to differentiate between "payment needed" (-32402) and "payment attempt rejected" (-32401).

section.name: Payment Challenge
section.t: The payment challenge is returned in error.data for -32402 and -32401 errors as a base64url-encoded JSON string. The decoded structure mirrors the HTTP WWW-Authenticate parameters from [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment].
Padding characters ("=") MUST NOT be included in the base64url encoding.

section.name: Challenge Object
section.t: When decoded, the challenge contains:

section.name: Required Fields
section.t: id (string) : Unique identifier for this challenge. Servers MUST generate a cryptographically random value with at least 128 bits of entropy. Clients MUST include this value in the credential.
realm (string) : Protection space identifier. Defines the scope of the payment requirement. Clients MAY use this to determine whether cached authorizations apply.
method (string) : Payment method identifier (e.g., "tempo", "lightning", "solana"). MUST be a lowercase ASCII string registered in the HTTP Payment Methods registry.
intent (string) : Payment intent type. Values defined in [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment]: - "charge": One-time payment - "approval": Pre-authorization for future charges
request (string) : Base64url-encoded JSON containing payment-method-specific data. Structure is defined by the payment method specification. Padding characters ("=") MUST NOT be included.

section.name: Optional Fields
section.t: expires (string) : RFC 3339 timestamp indicating when this challenge expires. Clients MUST NOT submit credentials for expired challenges.
description (string) : Human-readable description of the payment purpose. For display only; MUST NOT be relied upon for verification (see Security Considerations).

section.name: Payment Credential
section.t: Clients submit payment credentials by including an authorization field at the root level of the JSON-RPC request object.

section.name: Request Structure
section.t: The authorization field:

ul.li: MUST be a string
MUST begin with "Payment " (case-sensitive, with trailing space)
MUST be followed by a base64url-encoded credential

section.name: Credential Format
section.t: The credential is a base64url-encoded JSON object. The structure is defined by [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment] Section 5.2:

ul.li: id: MUST match the challenge id
payload: Payment-method-specific proof (e.g., signature, preimage)

section.name: Payment Receipt
section.t: On successful payment verification, servers MAY include a receipt field at the root level of the response object.

section.name: Receipt Format
section.t: The receipt format is defined by [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment] Section 5.3. Receipts provide proof of payment but are not cryptographic proofs of settlement (see Security Considerations).

section.name: Payment Authorization
section.t: Servers MAY issue a paymentAuthorization field to allow credential reuse across multiple requests, reducing payment overhead for high-frequency interactions.

section.name: Authorization Object
section.t: The paymentAuthorization is a base64url-encoded JSON object. When decoded, it contains:
realm (string, REQUIRED) : The protection space where this authorization is valid.
expires (string, REQUIRED) : RFC 3339 timestamp after which the authorization is invalid.
Padding characters ("=") MUST NOT be included in the base64url encoding.

section.name: Using Cached Authorizations
section.t: When a client has a valid paymentAuthorization:

ol.li: Client includes the original credential in authorization
Server verifies the credential and checks authorization cache
If valid, server processes request without re-settling payment

section.t: Servers MUST be able to revoke authorizations before expiry. When revoked, servers return -32402 or -32401 with a fresh challenge.

section.name: MCP Notifications
section.t: MCP notifications are JSON-RPC requests without an id field that expect no response. Since payment challenges require a response, notifications cannot support payment flows.

section.name: Server Behavior
section.t: Servers MUST NOT process payment-gated tools invoked via notifications. Servers SHOULD silently drop such notifications (per JSON-RPC 2.0 spec).
Servers MAY log dropped payment-required notifications for debugging.

section.name: Client Guidance
section.t: Clients SHOULD NOT invoke payment-gated tools as notifications. The tools/call method should always include a request id to receive payment challenges and results.

section.name: Transport Considerations
section.t: MCP supports multiple transport mechanisms. This section defines transport-specific considerations for payment flows.

section.name: Streamable HTTP Transport
section.t: When MCP uses Streamable HTTP transport:

ul.li: Implementations MUST use TLS 1.3 or later
Servers MAY use HTTP Payment scheme [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment]

section.t: instead of or in addition to this MCP extension

ul.li: The authorization field is independent of HTTP Authorization header
Payment challenges MAY be returned via HTTP 402 or MCP -32402 error

section.name: stdio Transport
section.t: When MCP uses stdio transport (local MCP servers):

ul.li: Trust is established by the process execution context
Payment credentials still provide cryptographic proof of authorization
Implementations SHOULD still validate credentials
This transport is common for local tool providers (e.g., filesystem,

section.t: database tools) which typically don't require payment

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: This specification inherits security considerations from [draft-ietf-httpauth-payment] Section 12. Additional MCP-specific considerations follow.

section.name: Credential Exposure in Logs
section.t: MCP messages are often logged for debugging. Implementations:

ul.li: MUST NOT log the authorization field value
MUST NOT log the receipt field value
SHOULD redact or omit these fields in debug output
MAY log field presence without values (e.g., "authorization": "[REDACTED]")

section.name: AI Agent Autonomy
section.t: MCP clients are often AI agents that may make payment decisions autonomously. Implementations SHOULD:

ul.li: Allow users to set spending limits per tool, realm, or session
Require user confirmation for payments above a threshold
Provide clear audit logs of all payments made
Support revoking agent payment capabilities

section.name: Error Information Leakage
section.t: Payment challenge errors (-32402, -32401) may reveal:

ul.li: Which tools require payment
Payment amounts and recipients
Server pricing structures

section.t: This information exposure is intentional for protocol operation. Servers concerned about pricing disclosure should consider alternative access control mechanisms.

section.name: Replay Across Transports
section.t: A credential generated for an HTTP request should not be replayable over MCP (and vice versa) if challenge IDs are properly scoped.
Servers MUST:

ul.li: Bind challenge id values to the transport/endpoint that issued them
Reject credentials with challenge IDs from different endpoints

section.name: Notification Abuse
section.t: Attackers may flood servers with payment-gated notifications to:

ul.li: Cause silent tool invocation failures
Mask legitimate notification traffic

section.t: Servers SHOULD rate limit notifications from untrusted clients.

section.name: MCP Error Code Registration
section.t: This document requests registration of the following error codes in a future MCP error code registry:
Note: JSON-RPC 2.0 reserves -32700 to -32600 for protocol errors and -32099 to -32000 for server errors. The codes -32402 and -32401 fall outside these ranges, in the implementation-defined space.

section.name: Examples
section.t: All examples use MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool call format.

section.name: Tool Discovery
section.t: Clients discover available tools via tools/list. Payment requirements are not exposed during discovery—they are returned when invoking payment-gated tools.
Request:
Response:

section.name: Payment Required Flow
section.t: Request (no authorization):
Response (payment required):
The decoded data contains:
Request (with authorization):
Response (success with receipt):

section.name: Payment with Authorization Reuse
section.t: When a server wants to allow credential reuse (e.g., for session-based billing), it includes paymentAuthorization in the response.
Response (success with authorization):
The decoded paymentAuthorization contains:
Subsequent request (reusing credential):
The server validates the cached authorization without re-settling payment.

section.name: Payment Failed
section.t: When a credential is well-formed but verification fails, the server returns -32401 with a fresh challenge.
Response (payment failed):

section.name: Text Content Response
section.t: Tools returning text content follow MCP's content array format.
Request:
Response:

section.name: Resource Link Response
section.t: Tools may return resource links for additional context.
Response:

section.name: Structured Output
section.t: Tools with outputSchema return structured content.
Response:

section.name: Tool Execution Error (Non-Payment)
section.t: Tool execution errors use isError: true, distinct from payment errors.
Response:
Note: Payment was successful (no -32402/-32401), but the tool itself failed. The payment is not refunded in this case—refund policies are payment-method-specific.

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: This specification builds on the HTTP Payment scheme designed for HTTP 402 semantics. Thanks to the contributors of that specification.
